title: Gonocarpus mezianus
taxonomy: Gonocarpus mezianus is a species of flowering plant in the family Haloragaceae. It is native to Australia. The classification of Gonocarpus mezianus includes its kingdom as Plantae, phylum as Angiospermophyta, class as Magnoliopsida, order as Haloragales, family as Haloragaceae, genus as Gonocarpus, and species as G. mezianus. This plant is also known as the spotted watermilfoil or spotted water-milfoil. Its classification helps in identifying and understanding the characteristics and behavior of the plant.

intro: Gonocarpus mezianus, also known as Australian swamp sedge, is a type of aquatic plant that belongs to the family Haloragaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in Australian Aboriginal medicine, and its health benefits include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are thought to aid in wound healing and the management of skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Gonocarpus mezianus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
